title: Oscar 2026 Best Picture Predictions
text: Scott Feinberg predicts Paul Thomas Anderson's film will triumph, noting it has dominated the precursor awards from the Directors Guild and Producers Guild to the Globes, Critics Choice and BAFTAs. Though Sinners received a record 16 nominations, One Battle After Another maintains stronger momentum across major voting bodies. Chief film critic David Rooney advocates for the same film, calling it wildly entertaining but also timely as it taps into collective discontent with creeping authoritarianism. He acknowledges The Secret Agent represents the most complex filmmaking, yet recognizes its international thriller status makes a sweep unlikely given Oscar voting patterns. Key contenders include Sinners with 16 nominations showing strong ensemble cast with Ryan Coogler's potential breakthrough, Hamnet as a sleeper candidate with popular appeal, and The Secret Agent as critics' darling but facing an uphill battle for major wins. The analysis reflects a split between predictive certainty and critical merit, a familiar tension in awards season forecasting.
